blurb:
This is the place we hit when we need breakfast at 2am or want something reliable any time of day. It's a classic diner with that neon sign you've probably driven past a million times on Cobb Parkway. Perfect for late night munchies, family breakfast on weekends, or when you can't decide what you want because the menu has everything.

The breakfast is what keeps people coming back. Get the big breakfast platter if you're really hungry, or try some of the Greek specialties since they do those well too. The portions are huge so you'll probably have leftovers.

Being open 24 hours is the real selling point here. It's nice knowing there's always somewhere decent to go no matter what time it is.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb: My wife discovered this place and now we're here at least twice a month. It's in a shopping center, but don't let that fool you. The pizza is legitimately some of the best I've had outside of Italy. Thin crust that's crispy but not cracker-like, and they use really good ingredients. The Margherita is perfect if you want to taste what they're all about. Simple but done right with fresh mozzarella and basil. The Prosciutto di Parma is my personal favorite. Salty, creamy, and just rich enough. Great for date night or grabbing a quick dinner with the family. Fair warning, it gets busy on weekends so either go early or be ready to wait a bit.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat because it's basically twenty different restaurants under one roof. Right near the square so you can walk around downtown after. Good for families with picky eaters or when you want options.

Every time I go I find something new to try. The variety is the whole point here so you can get tacos while your spouse gets Asian food and the kids get pizza. It's like a fancy food court but with local vendors and better quality.

Parking can be tricky downtown but it's worth dealing with. The atmosphere is lively and it feels like you're supporting a bunch of small local businesses at once.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This is our go-to when we want Middle Eastern food without breaking the bank. It's in a shopping center but the food is legit and the portions are generous. Good for a casual lunch or dinner when you want something different but still filling.

The shawarma and falafel are what most people order, and both are solid choices. They do a nice job with the veggie options too if you have people in your group with different dietary needs. Everything comes with good sides and the prices are really reasonable.

It's the kind of place where you can feed a family without spending a fortune. Nothing fancy about the atmosphere but the food speaks for itself.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb: This is where we go for anniversaries or when my parents visit and want somewhere special. The deck overlooking the Chattahoochee is gorgeous, especially at sunset, and the inside feels romantic without being stuffy. It's definitely a splurge, but worth it for the right occasion. The seafood here is what you come for. Their crab cakes are huge and actually full of crab, not filler. The shrimp and grits is rich and satisfying if you want something more Southern. Service is always on point, which matters when you're spending this much. Just make a reservation, especially if you want a river view table.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is the real deal French bakery that makes you feel like you're not in suburban Georgia. Perfect for weekend brunch or when you want to grab some quality pastries and coffee. The kind of place you take visiting friends to show off Marietta's food scene.

The baguettes are legit and the quiche is what I always end up ordering for brunch. Their petits fours are beautiful if you need something for a special occasion. Everything tastes like it was made by someone who actually knows French baking.

Get there early on weekends because the good stuff goes fast. They close early on Sundays so don't plan a late brunch here.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
If you've never done the all-you-can-eat Brazilian barbecue thing, this is a good place to try it. They bring the meat around on skewers and you eat until you can't anymore. It's fun for celebrations or when you have out-of-town guests who want to try something different.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip, and the feijoada stew is really good if you want to try traditional Brazilian comfort food. They also do breakfast which is unusual for this style of restaurant. The meat keeps coming so pace yourself.

It's in a strip mall but the inside is nice enough for a special dinner. Just come hungry because you're going to eat way more than you planned.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb: This is hands down one of the best dinner spots in Atlanta. My girlfriend brought me here for girls' night and I've been back three times since. The vibe is upscale but comfortable, perfect for a special dinner or when you want to try something different from the usual rotation. The chicken shish kabobs are incredible. Tender, perfectly seasoned, and come with this amazing rice with lentils and raisins that I could eat by itself. The hummus and wings are great for sharing if you're with a group. Don't skip the cocktails. They know what they're doing behind the bar. Just know they close between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb: This place absolutely lives up to the hype and then some. The atmosphere is vibrant without being loud, and the staff actually knows what they're talking about when you ask questions. Perfect for a fun night out with friends or when you want Mexican food that's a step above the usual spots. Start with the Esquite. It's street corn that's packed with flavor and sets the tone for everything else. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and strong enough to share with the table. I love that they balance the alcohol and sweetness just right, not too sugary like some places. They're open until midnight most nights, which is clutch when you want good food late. Just know it gets busy, especially on weekends.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb: This is my go-to spot when I'm craving proper Indian food and don't want to mess around. It's in a shopping center in Dunwoody, but inside it's colorful and lively with families sharing big platters of food. Perfect for when you want to try something different or need to feed a group without breaking the bank. The biryani is what they're known for and it's exactly what you'd hope for. Rich, fragrant rice with tender meat that comes out steaming hot. My kids even like the milder dishes here, which says something. Just know they serve everything family-style, so come hungry or ready to take leftovers home.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
